introduction: This document provides an overview of the risk readiness audits conducted by the asset management team at Elexion Automotive. The purpose is to evaluate our preparedness for the upcoming annual enterprise risk assessment review. Focus areas include cross-facility risk readiness, regulatory compliance, and asset management strategies.

subsections.heading: Cross-Facility Risk Management Strategies
subsections.content: Elexion Automotive's cross-facility risk management strategies have evolved significantly since 2018. We've seen a 30% reduction in risk-related incidents due to the implementation of advanced predictive analytics. Our strategies now prioritize the identification and mitigation of both physical and cyber vulnerabilities, ensuring a holistic approach to risk management.
subsections.justification: This content discusses risk management strategies but does not mention audits or timelines, which are crucial to the DR question.

subsections.heading: Regulatory Compliance and Cross-Border Challenges
subsections.content: Navigating cross-border regulations is a complex task for Elexion Automotive. In the past year, we have encountered an increase in regulatory requirements by 15%, particularly in the context of emissions standards and safety protocols. Our legal and compliance team continues to work diligently to ensure we meet these standards in all operating regions.
subsections.justification: The content discusses general compliance challenges, not specific to risk readiness audits or the 2026 target date.

subsections.heading: Asset Management Audit Overview
subsections.content: Our asset management audit process has been refined over time to maximize efficiency and effectiveness. In 2020, we introduced an upgraded audit software that improved our audit completion rate by 20%. These audits cover a wide range of necessities, from ensuring the proper maintenance of physical assets to validating the security of our digital infrastructure.
subsections.justification: The content discusses general audit processes and improvements, not related to the specific audits or the 2026 deadline.

conclusion: The asset management team has made considerable progress in conducting risk readiness audits across our facilities. However, there are areas for improvement in terms of regulatory compliance and cross-border challenges. Moving forward, we recommend increasing our focus on these areas to further strengthen our risk management strategies. This will help us prepare effectively for the upcoming annual enterprise risk assessment review.
